perturbation.original_text: Buyer Affiliates shall pay ExxonMobil's Selling Affiliates invoice(s) not later than the days set forth in Attachment E hereto. All invoices shall be paid in full by wire transfer in accordance with the invoice's instructions.
perturbation.changed_text: Buyer Affiliates shall pay ExxonMobil's Selling Affiliates invoice(s) not later than the days set forth in Attachment E hereto. However, Buyer Affiliates shall have the option to extend the payment deadline by an additional thirty (30) days, subject to a late payment fee as described in Attachment A. All invoices shall be paid in full by wire transfer in accordance with the invoice's instructions.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces an in-text contradiction by allowing Buyer Affiliates to extend payment deadlines, which conflicts with the original requirement to pay invoices within the timeframe outlined in Attachment E. This creates uncertainty and potential disputes over the enforceability of the original payment terms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: West Pharmaceutical Services and ExxonMobil Chemical Company 2019-2023 Global Supply Master Agreement Payment Terms Buyer Affiliates shall pay ExxonMobil Selling Affiliates' invoice(s) in full within the days set forth below: Location Payment Terms Delivery Terms - lncoterms 2010 Brazil [*****] [*****] China [*****] [*****] Denmark [*****] [*****] France [*****] [*****] Germany [*****] [*****] India [*****] [*****] Mexico [*****] [*****] Serbia [*****] [*****] Singapore [*****] [*****] U.S.A. [*****] [*****] Ireland [*****] [*****] * For Product sold and/or sourced from the US that is shipped overseas to a non-US location, title and risk of loss of Product shall transfer from ExxonMobil Chemical Company or any other ExxonMobil Seller to Buyer at the first point upon which the delivering marine vessel crosses the outer boundary of the United States Exclusive Economic Zone (EEZ). The EEZ extends 200 nautical miles beyond the coastal baseline defined in the United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea. For U.S. sales to Brazil or Mexico, Seller shall constitute the U.S. Principal Party in interest or Exporter for all purposes under applicable law.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction by allowing Buyer Affiliates to extend payment deadlines, which directly conflicts with the original requirement to pay invoices within the timeframe outlined in Attachment E, as described in the Contradicted Text: "Buyer Affiliates shall pay ExxonMobil Selling Affiliates' invoice(s) in full within the days set forth below: Location Payment Terms Delivery Terms - lncoterms 2010 Brazil [*****] [*****]". The addition of a potential 30-day extension directly undermines the defined payment terms in Attachment E for specific locations, creating an inconsistency in payment obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Product shall conform to ExxonMobil Selling Affiliates' standard sales specifications as of the date of shipment. Sellers agrees to support the creation of purchasing specifications for Products by Buyer and agrees to supply Products in accordance to those purchasing specifications. Seller has the right to review each purchasing specification and needs to accept in writing before such purchasing specifications become effective. Seller has the right to take exceptions to terms and provisions in these purchasing specifications if in contradiction with Seller's Sales specifications, General Terms and Condition of Sales or other Seller's procedures and policies.
perturbation.changed_text: Product shall conform to ExxonMobil Selling Affiliates' standard sales specifications as of the date of shipment. Seller agrees to support the creation of purchasing specifications for Products by Buyer and agrees to supply Products in accordance to those purchasing specifications. Seller's acceptance of Buyer's purchasing specifications will be assumed unless Seller provides written objection within ten (10) business days of receiving the specifications. Seller has no right to take exceptions to terms and provisions in these purchasing specifications.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation creates an in-text contradiction regarding the Seller's ability to take exceptions to Buyer's purchasing specifications. Originally, the Seller had the right to take exceptions, but the modified text removes this right, creating a direct conflict. This will create an in-text contradiction with attachment A section 8, where there is a limited warranty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: THERE ARE NO WARRANTIES WHICH EXTEND BEYOND THE PRODUCT DESCRIPTION HEREIN, AND SELLER MAKES NO WARRANTY, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, OF FITNESS FOR PARTICULAR USE, MERCHANTABILITY OR OTHERWISE WITH RESPECT TO PRODUCT, WHETHER USED SINGLY OR IN COMBINATION WITH OTHER SUBSTANCES OR IN ANY PROCESS, EXCEPT THAT PRODUCT SOLD HEREUNDER SHALL CONFORM TO SELLER'S STANDARD SALES SPECIFICATIONS AS OF THE DATE OF SHIPMENT. Without limiting the foregoing, Seller does not recommend nor endorse the use of Product(s) in any medical application and specifically disclaims any representation or warranty, express or implied, of suitability or fitness for use, or otherwise with respect to Product(s)' use in any medical application. Buyer represents and warrants that no Product(s) purchased hereunder will be used in or resold into any commercial or developmental manner in connection with medical applications without Seller's prior express written acknowledgement. Further, Buyer agrees that it will make no representations, express or implied, to any person to the effect that Seller recommends or endorses the use of Product(s) purchased hereunder in any medical application.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed the Seller to take exceptions to Buyer's purchasing specifications if they contradicted the Seller's specifications. The changed text removes this right, stating that 'Seller has no right to take exceptions'. The Contradicted Text states that 'PRODUCT...SHALL CONFORM TO SELLER'S STANDARD SALES SPECIFICATIONS'. If the Seller cannot take exceptions to Buyer's purchasing specifications, and those specifications conflict with the Seller's, the product might not conform to the seller's standard sales specifications, contradicting the warranty outlined in the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: No modification of this Agreement shall be of any force or effect unless such modification is in writing, expressly designated as an amendment hereto and signed by the parties' duly authorized representatives; and no modification shall be effected by the acknowledgment or acceptance of purchase order forms containing terms or conditions at variance with those set forth herein.
perturbation.changed_text: No modification of this Agreement shall be of any force or effect unless such modification is in writing, expressly designated as an amendment hereto and signed by the parties' duly authorized representatives. However, modifications can also be made through email correspondence between authorized representatives, provided that both parties acknowledge and agree to the terms in the email.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces an in-text contradiction by stating that modifications to the agreement must be in writing and signed by authorized representatives, but then also allowing modifications to be made via email correspondence, as long as both parties acknowledge and agree to the terms. This contradicts the requirement of a formal, signed amendment and creates ambiguity regarding which method of modification is valid. This also goes against attachment A section 17.
perturbation.contradicted_text: No modification of this Agreement shall be of any force or effect unless such modification is in writing, expressly designated as an amendment hereto and signed by the parties' duly authorized representatives; and no modification shall be effected by the acknowledgment or acceptance of purchase order forms containing terms or conditions at variance with those set forth herein. Except as explicitly set forth in this Agreement, this Agreement and the transaction(s) specified herein can be cancelled only with both parties' written consent.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original and contradicted text state that modifications must be in writing and signed. The changed text then allows for modifications via email, which contradicts the original requirement for a signed, written amendment. This creates uncertainty about which method of modification is valid, as explained in the Perturbation Explanation, making the two sections incompatible and leading to a strong in-text contradiction.
